ogTitle: Wonboyn North vs Wondabyne: Climate Safety

heading: Wonboyn North vs Wondabyne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wonboyn North, New South Wales and Wondabyne, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wonboyn North has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Wondabyne (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wonboyn North or Wondabyne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wonboyn North has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Wondabyne scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wonboyn North scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Wondabyne scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wonboyn North or Wondabyne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wonboyn North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
